steps.title: Set up the intersection equation
steps.content:
The graphs intersect where $x-c=-4(x-8)^2$. Move everything to one side:
$$4x^2-64x+256+x-c=0$$
$$4x^2-63x+(256-c)=0$$

steps.title: Find the boundary value with Desmos
steps.content:
Two distinct intersections require the discriminant to be greater than $0$. First find the tangent boundary, where the discriminant equals $0$:
$$(-63)^2-4(4)(256-c)=0$$
Desmos returns $c=\frac{127}{16}\approx 7.9375$.

steps.title: Choose a value above the boundary
steps.content: At $c=\frac{127}{16}$ there is exactly one repeated solution, so that is not enough. To get two distinct real solutions, choose a listed value greater than $\frac{127}{16}$. Only $13$ works, so choice D is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '3': 3 < 7.9375, so discriminant < 0 — no real solutions at all. ✗
  • Choice B — '7': 7 < 7.9375, so discriminant < 0 — no real solutions. ✗
  • Choice C — '$\frac{127}{16}$': c = 127/16 makes discriminant exactly 0 — one repeated (not two distinct) solution. ✗
  • Choice D — '13': 13 > 7.9375, so discriminant > 0 — two distinct real solutions. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
